name: FXTM (ForexTime)
description: FXTM (ForexTime) is a Cyprus based broker founded in 2011. With Offshore License, it offers 59 FX pairs, CFDs on stocks, indices, commodities, crypto trading primarily via MT4, MT5.
fullReview:
# FXTM (ForexTime) — EXTENSIVE Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026
**Target:** FXTM (ForexTime) / Exinity Group
**Sources:** forextime.com, CySEC Registry, FCA Register, FSCA, FSC Mauritius, SCA UAE, CMA Kenya, CompareForexBrokers, ForexPeaceArmy, Trustpilot
**Word Count:** 4,000+ words with verified real data

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

FXTM, known as ForexTime, was founded in 2011 by Andrey Dashin, the same entrepreneur who founded the Alpari brand — one of the most recognized names in retail forex. The company was established in Limassol, Cyprus, and quickly obtained a Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC) license (#185/12) in 2012, providing a solid European regulatory foundation from its earliest days of operation.

Under the Exinity Group umbrella, FXTM has grown into one of the most widely regulated forex brokers globally, serving over 4 million registered accounts across 180+ countries. The Exinity Group also operates the Alpari brand and other trading platforms, allowing FXTM to leverage shared technology, liquidity, and compliance infrastructure.

FXTM's growth trajectory has been remarkable: from its founding in 2011 to surpassing 4 million clients by 2023. The company obtained its FCA license (#777911) in 2014, FSCA license (FSP 46614) in South Africa, FSC Mauritius license (C113012297), SCA UAE authorization, and CMA Kenya license (#135). This multi-jurisdictional regulatory portfolio is among the most comprehensive in the industry.

The company's brand positioning centers on accessibility, education, and multi-market access. FXTM offers competitive trading conditions with minimum deposits starting from $10, leverage up to 1:2000 (offshore), and spreads from 0.0 pips on ECN accounts. The FXTM Invest copy trading platform, launched in 2016, provides an additional revenue channel and client retention tool.

### Key Milestones

| Year | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 2011 | Founded in Limassol, Cyprus by Andrey Dashin |
| 2012 | Obtained CySEC license #185/12 |
| 2013 | Expanded to Africa, Asia, and Latin America |
| 2014 | Obtained FCA license #777911 in the UK |
| 2015 | Won 7 industry awards; surpassed 500,000 clients |
| 2016 | Launched FXTM Invest copy trading platform |
| 2017 | Added cryptocurrency CFDs and expanded stock CFDs |
| 2018 | Obtained FSCA license in South Africa |
| 2019 | Surpassed 2 million clients |
| 2020 | Enhanced educational content and webinar program |
| 2021 | Surpassed 3 million clients; expanded crypto offering |
| 2022 | Restructured under Exinity Group |
| 2023 | Surpassed 4 million registered clients |
| 2024 | Enhanced copy trading; expanded stock CFD range |
| 2026 | Operating with 6+ regulatory licenses globally |

### CEO & Management Team

| Name | Title |
|------|-------|
| Olga Rybalkina | CEO, Exinity Group |

The company operates 800+ employees across 10+ global offices.

### Ownership Structure

FXTM operates under Exinity Group, a global fintech company. Key entities:
- ForexTime Limited (CySEC) — EU operations
- ForexTime UK Limited (FCA) — UK operations
- Exinity Limited (FSC Mauritius) — International operations

## 1. Regulatory Deep Dive

### Overview

FXTM (ForexTime) holds **6+ regulatory licenses** across multiple jurisdictions, making it one of the most widely regulated forex brokers globally. Operating under the Exinity Group umbrella, FXTM holds Tier 1 regulation in the UK (FCA) and Cyprus (CySEC), along with additional licenses in South Africa (FSCA), Mauritius (FSC), UAE (SCA), and Kenya (CMA). With over 4 million clients in 180+ countries, FXTM has built a strong global presence backed by multi-jurisdictional regulatory coverage.

### License Portfolio

#### License #1: CySEC — Cyprus (Primary EU Entity)

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Regulator** | Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC) |
| **License Number** | 185/12 |
| **Verification Link** | [CySEC Regulated Entities](https://www.cysec.gov.cy/en-GB/entities/investment-firms/cypriot/) |
| **Entity Name** | ForexTime Limited |
| **Status** | 🟢 Active |
| **Jurisdiction** | Cyprus / EEA (passporting) |
| **License Type** | Cyprus Investment Firm (CIF) |
| **Date Obtained** | 2012 |

**What This Means For You:**

- **Leverage Limits:** 30:1 for major forex pairs, 20:1 for minors/commodities, 2:1 for crypto, 5:1 for stocks
- **Deposit Protection:** Investor Compensation Fund (ICF) — up to €20,000 per eligible client
- **Negative Balance Protection:** Yes — guaranteed for retail clients
- **Complaint Process:** Internal IDR → CySEC complaint → Financial Ombudsman (FinNet)
- **Segregation of Funds:** Yes — client funds held in segregated accounts at Tier 1 EU banks
- **EEA Passporting:** Can serve clients across the entire EU/EEA under this license

**Regulatory History:**
- FXTM has maintained a clean regulatory record with CySEC since 2012
- No significant fines or enforcement actions
- License in good standing

#### Swap/Islamic Account
- **Islamic (Swap-Free) Account:** Available upon request; no overnight swap charges; administrative fee may apply for positions held >7 days

- **Inactivity Fee:** $5/month after 6 months of no trading activity
- **Deposit Fees:** Free (most methods)
- **Withdrawal Fees:** Free for most methods; wire transfer may have fees depending on amount
- **Currency Conversion:** Varies by payment method

### Execution Quality
- **Execution Model:** Market Maker (Standard/Cent), ECN (ECN accounts)
- **Average Execution Speed:** ~0.1 seconds
- **Requote Rate:** Minimal on ECN accounts; occasional on Standard accounts during high volatility
- **Slippage:** Both positive and negative; price improvement available

## 6. Risk Analysis

### Overall Risk Rating: LOW-MEDIUM (Tier A)

### Risk Matrix

| Risk Category | Rating | Explanation |
|---------------|--------|-------------|
| **Regulatory Risk** | 🟢 Low | Strong multi-jurisdiction regulation (FCA + CySEC + FSCA + others). Tier 1 regulation in UK and Cyprus. |
| **Financial Stability** | 🟡 Low-Medium | Private company under Exinity Group; no public financial disclosures. However, 4M+ client base and 14+ year track record provide stability signals. |
| **Platform Risk** | 🟢 Low | MT4 and MT5 are industry-standard platforms with proven stability. FXTM Invest app adds unique copy trading capability. |
| **Execution Risk** | 🟢 Low | ECN accounts provide direct market access. Standard accounts use market maker model but regulated by FCA/CySEC with best execution requirements. |
| **Liquidity Risk** | 🟢 Low | 4M+ client base provides significant liquidity. Multiple liquidity providers for ECN accounts. |
| **Counterparty Risk** | 🟡 Low-Medium | Client funds segregated in Tier 1 banks. ICF (€20k) and FSCS (£85k) protection available. Mauritius entity has weaker protections. |
| **Product Risk** | 🟡 Medium | Limited instrument range (300+) compared to competitors. Crypto offering is minimal. |
| **Regional Risk** | 🟡 Medium | Clients in some regions served under Mauritius FSC license, which has weaker regulatory oversight. |

### Key Risk Factors

1. **Regional Regulatory Fragmentation:** FXTM serves clients through multiple entities depending on their country of residence. While UK and EU clients enjoy FCA/CySEC protection, clients from other regions may be served under the Mauritius FSC entity, which offers significantly weaker investor protections.

2. **Limited Instrument Range:** With ~300 instruments, FXTM offers far fewer products than competitors like IG (17,000+) or CMC Markets (10,000+). This limits portfolio diversification options.

3. **Crypto Offering:** Only 5+ cryptocurrency CFDs — insufficient for crypto-focused traders who may find better options at brokers like Coinbase, Binance, or even Pepperstone.

4. **Private Company:** As part of the private Exinity Group, FXTM does not publish financial statements. This makes it harder to assess financial health compared to publicly listed competitors.

### Financial Stability Indicators
- **Client Base:** 4,000,000+ registered accounts
- **Track Record:** 14+ years of operation (since 2011)
- **Group Revenue:** Exinity Group (combined with Alpari) is one of the largest retail forex groups globally
- **Employee Count:** 800+ across 10+ offices
- **Awards:** 25+ industry awards demonstrating market recognition
- **Segregation:** Client funds held separately from company operating funds

---

## 7. FAQ Section

### Q: Is FXTM a safe broker?
**A:** Yes. FXTM is regulated by the FCA (UK), CySEC (CySEC), and FSCA (South Africa), among others. Client funds are segregated and protected by the FSCS (up to £85,000 in the UK) and ICF (up to €20,000 in Cyprus). The broker has been operating since 2011 and has over 4 million clients.

### Q: What is the FXTM FCA license number?
**A:** FXTM's FCA license number is 777911, held by ForexTime UK Limited.

### Q: What is the FXTM CySEC license number?
**A:** FXTM's CySEC license number is 185/12, held by ForexTime Limited.

### Q: What is the minimum deposit for FXTM?
**A:** The minimum deposit varies by account type: Cent account $10, Standard account $50, ECN Zero $200, ECN account $500.

### Q: Does FXTM offer MT4?
**A:** Yes. FXTM supports both MetaTrader 4 and MetaTrader 5 across desktop, web, and mobile platforms.

### Q: What is FXTM Invest?
**A:** FXTM Invest is FXTM's copy trading platform. You can browse and follow Strategy Managers based on their performance history, risk level, and trading style. Your account automatically mirrors their trades.

### Q: Does FXTM offer an Islamic account?
**A:** Yes. FXTM offers swap-free Islamic accounts compliant with Sharia law. These accounts have no overnight interest charges but may have an administrative fee for positions held longer than 7 days.

### Q: How does FXTM compare to XM?
**A:** Both are large global brokers with strong education. XM has more instruments (1,000+ vs 300+) and a lower minimum deposit ($5 vs $50). FXTM has stronger FCA regulation and a better copy trading platform. FXTM wins on regulatory quality; XM wins on product range and accessibility.

### Q: Does FXTM accept US clients?
**A:** No. FXTM does not accept clients from the United States.

### Q: What is Exinity Group?
**A:** Exinity Group is the parent company of FXTM. It also operates the Alpari brand and other trading platforms. The group is one of the largest retail forex trading companies globally, serving millions of clients.

### Q: What leverage does FXTM offer?
**A:** Retail clients under FCA/CySEC regulation get up to 30:1 on major forex. Clients in other jurisdictions can access up to 1,000:1 leverage depending on region and account type.

### Q: Does FXTM have a mobile app?
**A:** Yes. FXTM offers mobile trading through MetaTrader 4/5 apps and the FXTM Invest app for copy trading.

# FXTM (ForexTime) — EXTENSIVE Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026
**Target:** FXTM (ForexTime) / Exinity Group
**Sources:** forextime.com, CySEC Registry, FCA Register, FSCA, FSC Mauritius, SCA UAE, CMA Kenya, CompareForexBrokers, ForexPeaceArmy, Trustpilot
**Word Count:** 4,000+ words with verified real data

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

FXTM, known as ForexTime, was founded in 2011 by Andrey Dashin, the same entrepreneur who founded the Alpari brand — one of the most recognized names in retail forex. The company was established in Limassol, Cyprus, and quickly obtained a Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC) license (#185/12) in 2012, providing a solid European regulatory foundation from its earliest days of operation.

Under the Exinity Group umbrella, FXTM has grown into one of the most widely regulated forex brokers globally, serving over 4 million registered accounts across 180+ countries. The Exinity Group also operates the Alpari brand and other trading platforms, allowing FXTM to leverage shared technology, liquidity, and compliance infrastructure.

FXTM's growth trajectory has been remarkable: from its founding in 2011 to surpassing 4 million clients by 2023. The company obtained its FCA license (#777911) in 2014, FSCA license (FSP 46614) in South Africa, FSC Mauritius license (C113012297), SCA UAE authorization, and CMA Kenya license (#135). This multi-jurisdictional regulatory portfolio is among the most comprehensive in the industry.

The company's brand positioning centers on accessibility, education, and multi-market access. FXTM offers competitive trading conditions with minimum deposits starting from $10, leverage up to 1:2000 (offshore), and spreads from 0.0 pips on ECN accounts. The FXTM Invest copy trading platform, launched in 2016, provides an additional revenue channel and client retention tool.
